feature.name: FileMaker Frontend Removal
feature.description: Remove all FileMaker dependencies from the frontend, transitioning to pure Supabase + Backend API architecture

findings.title: ENVIRONMENT_TYPES and FileMaker environment checks still present in production code
findings.description: TSK0015 states 'Remove ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER and AUTH_METHODS.FILEMAKER constants' and 'Simplify to single Supabase + Backend API path'. However, ENVIRONMENT_TYPES is still exported from dataService.js and actively used in useCustomer.js (lines 99-113) and CustomerForm.jsx (line 295) to conditionally route between FileMaker and backend processing. The getEnvironmentContext() function still returns ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.WEBAPP and includes deprecated FileMaker routing logic.
findings.recommendation: Remove all ENVIRONMENT_TYPES usage and conditional FileMaker routing from production code. Replace getEnvironmentContext() calls with getAuthenticationContext(). Remove the FileMaker code paths from useCustomer.js (lines 99-107) and CustomerForm.jsx (lines 294-297, 378-415, 790+). Update dataService.js to remove ENVIRONMENT_TYPES export entirely or mark it as deprecated with no functional usage.

findings.title: FileMaker legacy data transformation functions still active
findings.description: TSK0015 requires simplifying to a single routing path, but production code in useCustomer.js still imports and can execute 'transformBackendToFileMaker' and 'processCustomerData' (FileMaker-specific processing). The conditional logic at line 99-113 routes to FileMaker processing when env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER, even though this should never occur in a Supabase-only architecture.
findings.recommendation: Remove imports and usage of transformBackendToFileMaker and processCustomerData from useCustomer.js. Remove the conditional environment check (lines 99-113) and always use processBackendCustomerList. Either delete FileMaker transformation functions or move them to a deprecated utilities file with clear warnings.

findings.title: AppStateContext retains FileMaker UI state management
findings.description: TSK0021 and TSK0025 require removing FileMaker references and finalizing the migration. However, AppStateContext.jsx (lines 20, 53, 71, 75, 180-226) still contains 'showFileMakerExample' state, 'SET_SHOW_FILEMAKER_EXAMPLE' action, and FileMaker-related state clearing logic. This suggests UI components may still have FileMaker display modes.
findings.recommendation: Remove showFileMakerExample state, SET_SHOW_FILEMAKER_EXAMPLE action type, and all related reducer logic from AppStateContext.jsx. Search for components that consume this state (likely in examples or demos) and either remove them or update them to use backend-only patterns.

findings.title: CustomerForm contains extensive legacy FileMaker compatibility code
findings.description: TSK0004 claims full backend integration, but CustomerForm.jsx contains significant FileMaker-specific code: line 13 comment mentions 'FileMaker flat model', lines 37-123 have 'Legacy FileMaker fields', lines 294-297 and 378-415 have FileMaker environment conditional logic, and line 789+ has a collapsible 'Legacy FileMaker Fields' UI section. This violates the requirement to remove FileMaker code after backend migration completion.
findings.recommendation: Remove all FileMaker-specific form fields, validation logic, and UI sections from CustomerForm.jsx. Remove the ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER conditional at lines 294-297. Remove the FileMaker flat format handling at lines 378-415. Remove the 'Legacy FileMaker Fields' collapsible section (line 789+). Update JSDoc comments to remove references to FileMaker flat model.

findings.title: Dead code execution - FileMaker environment path unreachable
findings.description: useCustomer.js contains extensive conditional logic (lines 99-107, 147-154, 176-194, 219-264, 419-426) that checks `env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER`, but `getEnvironmentContext()` is hardcoded to always return `ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.WEBAPP` (dataService.js:306). This means the FileMaker code paths are unreachable dead code that still executes environment checks, imports FileMaker transformation functions, and increases complexity unnecessarily. The dual-write logic in handleCustomerUpdate (lines 236-259) will never execute but still imports useSupabaseCustomer.
findings.recommendation: Remove all `if (env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER)` blocks from useCustomer.js. Remove imports of FileMaker-specific functions (processCustomerData, formatCustomerForFileMaker, transformBackendToFileMaker). Simplify to single code path using backend processing functions only. This will reduce bundle size, improve code clarity, and eliminate unnecessary imports.

findings.title: Inconsistent state mutations with potential race conditions
findings.description: CustomerForm.jsx performs multiple asynchronous operations in handleSubmit (lines 368-375, 438-447) using setTimeout with hardcoded 100ms delays to auto-select newly created customers. This pattern is fragile: (1) the delay is arbitrary and doesn't guarantee the customer is loaded, (2) errors in handleCustomerSelect are caught but silently logged, (3) the component may unmount before timeout fires causing potential memory leaks, (4) the timeout is not cleaned up if the component unmounts. Additionally, the FileMaker dual-write operations (lines 417-436) perform side effects without error boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Replace setTimeout pattern with proper async/await flow: `await loadCustomers(); if (result?.id) { await handleCustomerSelect(result.id).catch(err => console.warn(...)); }`. If delay is truly needed for state propagation, use useEffect cleanup pattern with ref tracking. Better: let the parent component handle selection after form closure rather than mixing concerns.

findings.title: Missing null checks and potential undefined access
findings.description: Multiple locations access nested object properties without null checks: (1) useCustomer.js line 476 directly accesses `customer.isActive || customer.is_active` without checking if customer exists in the filter, (2) CustomerForm.jsx lines 306-322 filters contacts without validating array structure could be malformed, (3) dataService.js line 44 returns `currentAuthentication?.user?.supabaseOrgID || null` but the interceptor at line 135 still logs warning when null rather than throwing early.
findings.recommendation: Add null-safe operators: (1) Update activeCustomers filter to use optional chaining, (2) Validate array structure before filtering in CustomerForm (check emails.length, phones.length, addresses.length before filter/map), (3) Consider throwing error in dataService interceptor for endpoints that require org ID rather than warning and continuing, or maintain a whitelist of endpoints that don't require it.

findings.title: Unreachable deprecated code still imports production dependencies
findings.description: dualWriteService.js is marked deprecated and verified unused (line 13: 'NOT USED in production code'), but remains in the codebase importing production modules (salesService line 46, api/tasks line 340, api/financialRecords line 268). The entire file is 417 lines of dead code that still gets bundled. All exported functions log deprecation warnings but still execute full logic including async operations, error handling, and rollback attempts.
findings.recommendation: Complete TSK0025 requirement by deleting src/services/dualWriteService.js entirely. File header (lines 37-41) states 'TSK0025: Complete removal of this file after validation' and TSK0025 is marked complete. If keeping for historical reference is required, move to a /deprecated folder outside src/ so it's not included in production bundle. Verify no dynamic imports reference this file.

findings.title: Supabase Service Role Key exposed in frontend bundle
findings.description: The Supabase service role key (VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) is being imported and exposed in the frontend bundle via config.js and supabaseService.js. The service role key bypasses ALL Row-Level Security (RLS) policies and grants unrestricted database access. This key should NEVER be in frontend code as it can be extracted from the compiled JavaScript bundle by any attacker. Lines 22, 29-30 in config.js export this key, and supabaseService.js (lines 10, 16, 42, 48, 57) uses it for 'admin operations'. Even though it's loaded from environment variables, Vite bundles these into the client-side JavaScript, making them accessible to anyone who inspects the application.
findings.recommendation: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: (1) Remove VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from all frontend code - this key must ONLY exist on the backend server. (2) Delete supabaseServiceRoleKey export from config.js. (3) Remove performAdminOperation and all service role key usage from supabaseService.js. (4) Any operations requiring service role key must be moved to backend API endpoints. (5) Rotate the service role key immediately after removal since it may already be exposed in deployed bundles. (6) Audit git history to ensure the actual key value was never committed. This is a critical security vulnerability that could allow complete database compromise.

findings.title: HMAC secret key exposed in frontend bundle
findings.description: The backend HMAC secret key (VITE_SECRET_KEY) used for API authentication is loaded in the frontend (dataService.js:61, supabaseService.js:65, config.js:15-16) and bundled into client-side JavaScript. While the code attempts to use it for generating HMAC signatures client-side, this is fundamentally insecure - anyone can extract the secret from the bundle and forge authenticated requests to the backend API. The fallback tokens ('dev-token', 'fallback-token') at lines 67, 74, 102 are also problematic as they suggest the backend might accept weak authentication. HMAC secrets must remain server-side only.
findings.recommendation: CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: (1) Remove VITE_SECRET_KEY from frontend environment variables entirely. (2) Redesign authentication to use JWT tokens issued by the backend after Supabase authentication, NOT client-side HMAC generation. (3) Backend should verify the user's Supabase JWT and issue a short-lived backend session token. (4) Remove generateBackendAuthHeader from frontend - authentication headers should come from backend-issued tokens. (5) Remove all fallback authentication modes ('dev-token', 'fallback-token') - these create security bypasses. (6) Rotate HMAC secret immediately. This vulnerability allows complete API authentication bypass.

findings.title: Broken separation of concerns - hooks contain business logic and data transformation
findings.description: The established architecture (CLAUDE.md lines 234-248) defines clear layer responsibilities: Hooks should 'encapsulate data operations and state management', Services should handle 'business logic and data processing', and API layer should handle 'direct API communication'. However, useCustomer.js violates this by implementing extensive business logic within the hook: environment detection (lines 93, 142, 174), conditional data processing (lines 99-113, 147-154, 186-194), and direct service function calls for transformations. The hook imports 10+ service functions (lines 12-20) and uses them conditionally based on environment, which should be handled at the service layer. This creates tight coupling between presentation logic and business logic, making the hook difficult to test and reuse.
findings.recommendation: Refactor to proper layered architecture: (1) Move all environment detection and conditional logic to the service layer. Create a single service function like `fetchAndProcessCustomers(options)` in customerService.js that handles environment detection internally and returns consistently formatted data. (2) Hook should only call one service function and manage React state, not choose between multiple processing functions. (3) Remove all ENVIRONMENT_TYPES imports from hooks - environment concerns belong in services. (4) Pattern should be: `const result = await customerService.fetchCustomers(options); setCustomers(result.customers); setPagination(result.pagination);` with NO conditional logic in the hook.

findings.title: Inconsistent architectural patterns across similar hooks
findings.description: The codebase shows three different architectural patterns for the same type of operation across hooks: (1) useCustomer.js (lines 88-128) uses environment detection IN the hook with conditional service calls, (2) useProject.js (lines 63-91) hardcodes 'backend' format and calls processProjectData with source parameter, avoiding environment detection entirely, and (3) useNote.js (lines 1-100) delegates ALL business logic to the service layer with no environment handling. This inconsistency violates the architectural principle that similar operations should follow similar patterns. The established pattern per CLAUDE.md is that hooks should be thin wrappers around service calls, yet we have three radically different implementations.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on Pattern C (useNote pattern) as the correct architecture: (1) All hooks should delegate to service layer without environment detection. (2) Services handle environment concerns internally. (3) Update useCustomer.js to follow useNote.js pattern: remove getEnvironmentContext, remove conditional logic, call single service function. (4) Update useProject.js to remove hardcoded 'backend' parameter - service should detect environment internally. (5) Create architectural decision record (ADR) documenting the standard hook pattern to prevent future inconsistencies. (6) Code review checklist should verify hooks don't contain business logic.

findings.title: Service layer violates single responsibility - transformBackendToFileMaker breaks abstraction
findings.description: The service layer architecture (CLAUDE.md line 239-242) should provide 'business logic and data processing' with 'API abstraction', yet customerService.js implements transformBackendToFileMaker (lines 34, 50, 100) which transforms backend data TO a deprecated FileMaker format. This violates single responsibility because: (1) The migration is complete (TSK0025 done), FileMaker is removed, yet services still output FileMaker format. (2) Services should provide domain models, not legacy wire formats. (3) The transformation happens AFTER the API call, meaning every consumer receives FileMaker-formatted data even in a pure backend architecture. (4) This creates unnecessary coupling to deprecated formats in the business layer.
findings.recommendation: Eliminate FileMaker format from service layer outputs: (1) Remove all transformBackendToFileMaker calls from processBackendCustomerList and processBackendCustomerDetail. (2) Services should return data in the backend/domain model format (business_name, is_active, etc.), not legacy FileMaker format (Name, f_active). (3) If any components require FileMaker format for backward compatibility, create a presentation-layer adapter/transformer in the component directory, not the service layer. (4) Update all consuming hooks/components to use backend field names. (5) Delete transformBackendToFileMaker utility after migration is verified complete. This aligns services with their defined responsibility of business logic, not format translation.

findings.title: API layer bypasses service layer - direct imports violate layered architecture
findings.description: The documented architecture (CLAUDE.md line 420) shows the flow as 'Component → Hook → Service → API Client → Backend API', establishing a strict layered architecture where each layer only communicates with adjacent layers. However, useCustomer.js (lines 3-10) imports API functions directly: `import { fetchCustomers, fetchCustomerById, ... } from '../api'`, bypassing the service layer entirely. The hook then calls API functions directly (line 94: `await fetchCustomers(options)`) and THEN calls service functions for transformation. This violates the architecture in two ways: (1) Hooks should not import from API layer - they should only import from services. (2) The pattern 'call API, then call service to process' inverts the dependency - services should encapsulate API calls.
findings.recommendation: Enforce strict layered architecture: (1) Remove ALL API imports from hooks. Hooks should only import from '../services'. (2) Create service functions that encapsulate the full operation: `customerService.fetchCustomers(options)` should call the API internally and return processed data. (3) Hook pattern should be: `import { fetchCustomers } from '../services/customerService'; const customers = await fetchCustomers(options);` - ONE function call that returns ready-to-use data. (4) Update CLAUDE.md to explicitly prohibit cross-layer imports. (5) Add ESLint rule to prevent hooks from importing from '../api' directory. This ensures proper separation of concerns and makes the codebase easier to test and modify.

findings.title: Dual-signature anti-pattern in useNote violates interface segregation
findings.description: useNote.js handleNoteCreate (lines 63-78) implements a 'dual-signature' pattern supporting both new signature (entityType, entityId, content, type) and legacy signature (entityId, content, type). The function uses complex runtime detection to determine which signature was used. This violates the Interface Segregation Principle and creates maintenance burden: (1) The migration is complete (TSK0025), there should be no 'legacy' code. (2) Runtime signature detection (lines 67-78) adds cognitive load and performance overhead. (3) Comments admit this is for 'backward compatibility' but don't specify what code uses the legacy signature. (4) This pattern will persist indefinitely unless explicitly removed.
findings.recommendation: Eliminate dual-signature pattern immediately: (1) Search codebase for ALL calls to handleNoteCreate and update to new signature. (2) Remove the signature detection logic (lines 67-78) and support ONLY the new signature. (3) Update JSDoc to remove 'backward compatibility' documentation. (4) If any calling code cannot be updated, create a separate deprecated function `handleNoteCreate_LEGACY` that calls the new signature internally, with deprecation warning. (5) This is a post-migration cleanup that should have been part of TSK0025. (6) Add to coding standards: 'No dual-signature functions - use function overloading via separate exports or default parameters for optional args'.

last_review.summary: Found 15 issue(s) requiring remediation - created 15 task(s)

standing_constraints: Do not remove FileMaker code until all feature backend integrations are verified working
Maintain backward compatibility during transition - use feature flags if needed
All changes must pass build and existing tests
Follow backend change request protocol - do not modify database schema
Update documentation as code is removed
Keep audit trail of what was removed and why

tasks.title: Verify all feature backend integrations complete
tasks.description: Check that all prerequisite feature migrations (customers, tasks, projects, notes, links, financial records, QuickBooks) have backend APIs deployed and functional. Teams is already complete (INS0026). Review deployment status and test basic API calls for each feature.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a full verification of backend integrations across prerequisite features by reviewing the OpenAPI spec, checking key API integration points for customers, projects, tasks, notes, links, financial records, QuickBooks, and teams, and confirming auth and endpoint availability. Added documentation outputs in 'BACKEND_INTEGRATION_VERIFICATION.md', '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/TSK0001_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md', and '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/README.md', and updated '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/tasks.json' to mark 'TSK0001' complete. Confirmed the project compiles cleanly via 'npm run build' and noted the key decision that several features are already backend-only while customers, projects, and tasks still retain FileMaker fallbacks for compatibility.

tasks.title: Audit FileMaker references in codebase
tasks.description: Create comprehensive inventory of all FileMaker references, categorized by: (1) API calls, (2) environment detection, (3) authentication, (4) configuration, (5) utilities, (6) tests. Document which references are safe to remove vs. which need replacement. Output to audit report file.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a comprehensive audit of all FileMaker references across the codebase and produced two new deliverables: 'FILEMAKER_AUDIT_REPORT.md' (inventory of 85 files, categorized into 6 reference types with per-file removal safety analysis, dependency graph, backend verification checklist, and a 6-phase removal plan) and 'AUDIT_SUMMARY.md' (executive stats, readiness assessment, risks, and next steps). Key decisions included categorizing references by integration pattern (API calls, environment detection, auth/config, utilities, UI, tests) and defining a phased deprecation approach that keeps core environment detection until the final phase while removing low-risk examples first. Verified report completeness and confirmed the build still succeeds after generating the documentation.

tasks.title: Create feature flag system for gradual rollout
tasks.description: Implement a feature flag mechanism to control FileMaker vs. Backend API routing during transition. Add useFeatureFlag hook and FeatureFlagProvider context. Flags should control: (1) use_filemaker_auth, (2) use_filemaker_customers, (3) use_filemaker_projects, etc. Allows safe incremental migration.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a feature flag system to support gradual rollout of backend API migrations, including a new FeatureFlagContext provider with localStorage persistence and CRUD operations plus hooks for basic access, environment-aware flag behavior, and declarative routing. Key files added or updated include 'src/context/FeatureFlagContext.jsx', 'src/hooks/useFeatureFlag.js', 'src/hooks/index.js', and integration of the FeatureFlagProvider into the app provider hierarchy in 'src/main.jsx' (placed after AppStateProvider). Added comprehensive documentation in 'docs/FEATURE_FLAGS.md' and 'docs/FEATURE_FLAG_MIGRATION_EXAMPLE.md', and updated 'CLAUDE.md' with usage patterns, available flags, and debugging notes; builds were run and confirmed successful to validate compilation.

tasks.title: Update customers API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/customers.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchCustomers, fetchCustomerById, createCustomer, updateCustomer, deleteCustomer functions. Handle new schema mapping, error handling, and pagination. Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented the switch from legacy '/contacts_api' routes to the real backend customer endpoints by updating 'src/api/customers.js' to call '/api/customers' for all CRUD operations (and aligning status toggling to use '/api/customers/{id}/status' with an 'is_active' boolean plus updating active-customer filtering to use 'active=true'), while confirming '/api/customers/search' was already correct. Updated 'src/services/dataService.js' to remap 'devCustomers' from 'contacts_api' to 'api/customers' so frontend requests resolve through the Vite '/api' proxy correctly. Kept the existing environment-aware routing to preserve FileMaker compatibility and noted the existing 'use_backend_customers' feature flag for future rollout rather than changing behavior now. Also recorded completion by updating 'tasks.json' and adding 'TSK0004_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md'.

tasks.title: Update projects API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/projects.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchProjects, fetchProjectById, createProject, updateProject, archiveProject functions. Handle new schema for objectives, images, and related data. Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented backend-ready projects API routing by updating the client in 'projects.js' to consistently use REST-style backend endpoints with the '/api' prefix (covering core project CRUD plus related objectives, steps, images, notes, status, and reorder operations) while retaining FileMaker fallback and existing org scoping and normalization. Fixed response handling in 'fetchProjectsForCustomers' to correctly unwrap array payloads when aggregating results across multiple customers. Deliberately kept feature flag logic out of the API layer after confirming hooks are not usable there, aligning with the established pattern in 'customers.js' where the 'use_backend_projects' rollout flag (default false) is enforced at the service or hook level. Added a completion note in '.devflow/tasks/projects-backend-integration/TSK0002_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md' and verified changes via a successful build.

tasks.title: Update tasks API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/tasks.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchTasks, fetchTaskById, createTask, updateTask, updateTaskStatus functions. Handle timer operations (start/stop/pause) using new backend timer endpoints. Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented TSK0006 by migrating the frontend tasks API to environment-aware backend endpoints using dataService instead of a hardcoded USE_BACKEND_API flag, while preserving the FileMaker fallback via getEnvironmentContext and ENVIRONMENT_TYPES. Updated src/api/tasks.js to route task CRUD through '/api/tasks' (including toggle-completion) and reworked timer operations to use the '/time-entries/*' pattern rather than '/tasks/{id}/timer/*', keeping data transformations and handleApiError consistency. Marked the work complete in '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/tasks.json' and added documentation in '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/TSK0006_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md' and '.devflow/tasks/filemaker-frontend-removal/TSK0006_QUICK_REFERENCE.md', with builds verified via 'npm run build'.

tasks.title: Update notes API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/notes.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchNotesByEntity, createNote, updateNote, deleteNote functions. Handle polymorphic associations (customer/project/task). Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Verified that TSK0007 was already complete: the notes API in 'src/api/notes.js' uses backend endpoints via 'dataService.get post patch delete' for project, task, and customer notes, with org scoping and no FileMaker-specific logic in the notes layer (environment routing remains centralized in 'dataService'). Confirmed supporting layers in 'src/services/noteService.js' (snake_case to camelCase, 'note' to 'content', backward-compatible 'fieldData') and 'src/hooks/useNote.js' (CRUD and per-entity pagination) are backend-ready, and validated feature flags 'use_backend_project_notes' and 'use_backend_task_notes' are enabled. Marked TSK0007 done in 'tasks.json' and added verification and reference docs ('TSK0007_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md', 'TSK0007_QUICK_REFERENCE.md', 'TSK0007_VERIFICATION_REPORT.md') after confirming the build passes.

tasks.title: Update links API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/links.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchLinksByEntity, createLink, updateLink, deleteLink functions. Handle polymorphic associations (customer/project/task). Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented full cleanup of remaining FileMaker legacy logic in the Links feature now that the API layer already targets backend '/links' endpoints. Updated 'src/services/linkService.js' to remove the FileMaker-only 'processLinks' formatter plus environment-context imports and conditional routing, and updated 'src/hooks/useLink.js' to drop environment checks and simplify create handling to backend responses only. Enabled the 'use_backend_links' flag by default in 'src/context/FeatureFlagContext.jsx', then verified the build, removed remaining FileMaker references, and added completion and verification docs while marking the task complete in 'tasks.json'.

tasks.title: Update financial records API to use backend endpoints
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker calls in src/api/financialRecords.js with backend API endpoints. Update fetchFinancialRecords, fetchUnpaidRecords, fetchRecordsByDateRange functions. Handle new customer_sales schema. Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented TSK0009 by migrating the financial records client API from direct Supabase RPC usage to backend REST endpoints under '/api/financial-records/*', routing all operations through 'dataService' with HMAC-authenticated requests. The primary change was in 'src/api/financialRecords.js', updating all fetch, create, summary, and mark-billed flows to call endpoints like '/query', '/unpaid', '/summary/{monthly|quarterly|yearly}', '/create', and '/mark-billed', and revising 'normalizeFinancialRecords()' to match the backend schema (including parsing numeric strings and supporting fields like 'project_id', 'billing_status', 'time_entry_id', and 'configuration_data') while preserving the legacy 'fieldData' wrapper. Important decisions included keeping backward compatibility by deprecating get-by-ID helpers (no backend endpoint available) with console warnings and fallback full-table scans, and relying on the already-enabled feature flag 'use_backend_financial_records'. Added completion docs ('TSK0009_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md', 'TSK0009_QUICK_REFERENCE.md') and marked the task complete in 'tasks.json', with builds verified successful.

tasks.title: Replace QuickBooks FileMaker scripts with backend API
tasks.description: Remove FileMaker script execution for QuickBooks (initializeQuickBooks, testConnection, etc.) from src/api/fileMaker.js. Replace with backend API calls to new QuickBooks endpoints. Update financial UI panels to use new integration points. Add feature flag support.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented the QuickBooks migration by removing the unused FileMaker-based 'initializeQuickBooks' function from 'src/api/fileMaker.js' and dropping its export from 'src/api/index.js', replacing it with exports for the backend QuickBooks API (status, unbilled records, invoice creation, sync, config, and customer search). Added a new feature flag 'use_backend_quickbooks' (default true) in 'src/context/FeatureFlagContext.jsx' to control rollout, and documented it in 'docs/FEATURE_FLAGS.md'. Created a completion and migration guide in 'TSK0010_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md' and marked the work complete in 'tasks.json', with a successful build confirming no breaking changes since the removed function had no callers.'

tasks.title: Update dualWriteService for Supabase-only mode
tasks.description: Refactor src/services/dualWriteService.js to remove FileMaker writes. Either deprecate entirely (if backend handles all writes) or update to single-write mode with logging. Add deprecation warnings. Document migration path.
tasks.implementation_notes: Deprecated dualWriteService.js by adding comprehensive deprecation notices to the file header explaining the service is obsolete now that backend API handles all writes (TSK0004-TSK0006 complete). Added @deprecated JSDoc tags and console.warn() calls to all exported functions: withDualWrite(), stopTimerWithDualWrite(), dualWriteConfig methods, and isDualWriteEnabled(). Created DUAL_WRITE_SERVICE_DEPRECATION.md with full migration paths showing how to use backend API endpoints instead. Updated CLAUDE.md to mark dualWriteService as deprecated. Verified via grep that no production code imports or uses this service. Build passes with npm run build. Service remains in codebase as deprecated reference until TSK0025 final cleanup.

tasks.title: Remove FileMaker reconciliation from financialSyncService
tasks.description: Update src/services/financialSyncService.js to remove FileMaker dapiRecords reconciliation logic. Update to rely solely on Supabase customer_sales as source of truth. Add migration validation if needed.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented TSK0012 by removing FileMaker reconciliation from financialSyncService, deleting 9 FileMaker-specific helpers (about 298 LOC) and refactoring synchronizeFinancialRecords and getFinancialSyncStatus to run in validation-only, Supabase-only mode with deprecation warnings directing developers to the Backend API. Key code changes were focused in the financialSyncService module (imports cleaned to retain only the essential query path) and tasks.json was updated to mark TSK0012 complete. Created new documentation artifacts including TSK0012_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md, TSK0012_QUICK_REFERENCE.md, and TSK0012_VERIFICATION_REPORT.md, and updated CLAUDE.md to reflect the migration guidance. Multiple npm run build checks confirmed no compilation errors and maintained backward compatibility while significantly reducing the service footprint (final file about 284 lines).

tasks.title: Remove FileMaker environment detection from initializationService
tasks.description: Update src/services/initializationService.js to remove FileMaker environment detection (window.FileMaker check). Remove FileMaker-specific initialization paths. Simplify to Supabase-only startup flow. Keep backward compatibility via feature flag initially.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented TSK0013 by stripping all FileMaker-specific initialization logic and environment readiness handling, making initialization Supabase-only while keeping shared methods like 'fetchSupabaseUserId', 'preloadData', and 'loadProducts'. Key changes were in 'initializationService.js' (removed 'waitForFileMaker', 'loadUserContext', 'delay', retry constants, and FileMaker imports) and 'index.jsx' (removed 'useFileMakerBridge' usage, FileMaker init block, readiness UI, and related effect dependencies). Added a deliberate deprecation path that detects 'ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER', logs a warning, sets a user-facing error, and aborts initialization. Updated 'tasks.json' to mark TSK0013 complete and added task docs ('TSK0013_COMPLETION_SUMMARY.md', 'TSK0013_QUICK_REFERENCE.md', 'TSK0013_VERIFICATION_REPORT.md') with successful build verification noted.

tasks.title: Remove FileMaker auth from SignIn component

tasks.name: [REVIEW] ENVIRONMENT_TYPES and FileMaker environment checks still present in production code
tasks.title: [REVIEW] ENVIRONMENT_TYPES and FileMaker environment checks still present in production code
requirements: TSK0015 states 'Remove ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER and AUTH_METHODS.FILEMAKER constants' and 'Simplify to single Supabase + Backend API path'. However, ENVIRONMENT_TYPES is still exported from dataService.js and actively used in useCustomer.js (lines 99-113) and CustomerForm.jsx (line 295) to conditionally route between FileMaker and backend processing. The getEnvironmentContext() function still returns ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.WEBAPP and includes deprecated FileMaker routing logic.

**Recommendation:** Remove all ENVIRONMENT_TYPES usage and conditional FileMaker routing from production code. Replace getEnvironmentContext() calls with getAuthenticationContext(). Remove the FileMaker code paths from useCustomer.js (lines 99-107) and CustomerForm.jsx (lines 294-297, 378-415, 790+). Update dataService.js to remove ENVIRONMENT_TYPES export entirely or mark it as deprecated with no functional usage.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] FileMaker legacy data transformation functions still active
tasks.title: [REVIEW] FileMaker legacy data transformation functions still active
requirements: TSK0015 requires simplifying to a single routing path, but production code in useCustomer.js still imports and can execute 'transformBackendToFileMaker' and 'processCustomerData' (FileMaker-specific processing). The conditional logic at line 99-113 routes to FileMaker processing when env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER, even though this should never occur in a Supabase-only architecture.

Remove the conditional environment check (lines 99-113) and always use processBackendCustomerList. Either delete FileMaker transformation functions or move them to a deprecated utilities file with clear warnings.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] AppStateContext retains FileMaker UI state management
tasks.title: [REVIEW] AppStateContext retains FileMaker UI state management

tasks.name: [REVIEW] CustomerForm contains extensive legacy FileMaker compatibility code
tasks.title: [REVIEW] CustomerForm contains extensive legacy FileMaker compatibility code

Remove the FileMaker flat format handling at lines 378-415. Remove the 'Legacy FileMaker Fields' collapsible section (line 789+). Update JSDoc comments to remove references to FileMaker flat model.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COM-004 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Dead code execution - FileMaker environment path unreachable
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Dead code execution - FileMaker environment path unreachable
requirements: useCustomer.js contains extensive conditional logic (lines 99-107, 147-154, 176-194, 219-264, 419-426) that checks `env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER`, but `getEnvironmentContext()` is hardcoded to always return `ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.WEBAPP` (dataService.js:306). This means the FileMaker code paths are unreachable dead code that still executes environment checks, imports FileMaker transformation functions, and increases complexity unnecessarily. The dual-write logic in handleCustomerUpdate (lines 236-259) will never execute but still imports useSupabaseCustomer.

**Recommendation:** Remove all `if (env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER)` blocks from useCustomer.js. Remove imports of FileMaker-specific functions (processCustomerData, formatCustomerForFileMaker, transformBackendToFileMaker). Simplify to single code path using backend processing functions only. This will reduce bundle size, improve code clarity, and eliminate unnecessary imports.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COD-001 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Inconsistent state mutations with potential race conditions
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Inconsistent state mutations with potential race conditions
requirements: CustomerForm.jsx performs multiple asynchronous operations in handleSubmit (lines 368-375, 438-447) using setTimeout with hardcoded 100ms delays to auto-select newly created customers. This pattern is fragile: (1) the delay is arbitrary and doesn't guarantee the customer is loaded, (2) errors in handleCustomerSelect are caught but silently logged, (3) the component may unmount before timeout fires causing potential memory leaks, (4) the timeout is not cleaned up if the component unmounts. Additionally, the FileMaker dual-write operations (lines 417-436) perform side effects without error boundaries.

**Recommendation:** Replace setTimeout pattern with proper async/await flow: `await loadCustomers(); if (result?.id) { await handleCustomerSelect(result.id).catch(err => console.warn(...)); }`. If delay is truly needed for state propagation, use useEffect cleanup pattern with ref tracking. Better: let the parent component handle selection after form closure rather than mixing concerns.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COD-002 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing null checks and potential undefined access
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing null checks and potential undefined access
requirements: Multiple locations access nested object properties without null checks: (1) useCustomer.js line 476 directly accesses `customer.isActive || customer.is_active` without checking if customer exists in the filter, (2) CustomerForm.jsx lines 306-322 filters contacts without validating array structure could be malformed, (3) dataService.js line 44 returns `currentAuthentication?.user?.supabaseOrgID || null` but the interceptor at line 135 still logs warning when null rather than throwing early.

**Recommendation:** Add null-safe operators: (1) Update activeCustomers filter to use optional chaining, (2) Validate array structure before filtering in CustomerForm (check emails.length, phones.length, addresses.length before filter/map), (3) Consider throwing error in dataService interceptor for endpoints that require org ID rather than warning and continuing, or maintain a whitelist of endpoints that don't require it.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COD-003 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Unreachable deprecated code still imports production dependencies
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Unreachable deprecated code still imports production dependencies
requirements: dualWriteService.js is marked deprecated and verified unused (line 13: 'NOT USED in production code'), but remains in the codebase importing production modules (salesService line 46, api/tasks line 340, api/financialRecords line 268). The entire file is 417 lines of dead code that still gets bundled. All exported functions log deprecation warnings but still execute full logic including async operations, error handling, and rollback attempts.

**Recommendation:** Complete TSK0025 requirement by deleting src/services/dualWriteService.js entirely. File header (lines 37-41) states 'TSK0025: Complete removal of this file after validation' and TSK0025 is marked complete. If keeping for historical reference is required, move to a /deprecated folder outside src/ so it's not included in production bundle. Verify no dynamic imports reference this file.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COD-004 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Supabase Service Role Key exposed in frontend bundle
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Supabase Service Role Key exposed in frontend bundle
requirements: The Supabase service role key (VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) is being imported and exposed in the frontend bundle via config.js and supabaseService.js. The service role key bypasses ALL Row-Level Security (RLS) policies and grants unrestricted database access. This key should NEVER be in frontend code as it can be extracted from the compiled JavaScript bundle by any attacker. Lines 22, 29-30 in config.js export this key, and supabaseService.js (lines 10, 16, 42, 48, 57) uses it for 'admin operations'. Even though it's loaded from environment variables, Vite bundles these into the client-side JavaScript, making them accessible to anyone who inspects the application.

**Recommendation:** IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: (1) Remove VITE_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from all frontend code - this key must ONLY exist on the backend server. (2) Delete supabaseServiceRoleKey export from config.js. (3) Remove performAdminOperation and all service role key usage from supabaseService.js. (4) Any operations requiring service role key must be moved to backend API endpoints. (5) Rotate the service role key immediately after removal since it may already be exposed in deployed bundles. (6) Audit git history to ensure the actual key value was never committed. This is a critical security vulnerability that could allow complete database compromise.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding SEC-001 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] HMAC secret key exposed in frontend bundle
tasks.title: [REVIEW] HMAC secret key exposed in frontend bundle
requirements: The backend HMAC secret key (VITE_SECRET_KEY) used for API authentication is loaded in the frontend (dataService.js:61, supabaseService.js:65, config.js:15-16) and bundled into client-side JavaScript. While the code attempts to use it for generating HMAC signatures client-side, this is fundamentally insecure - anyone can extract the secret from the bundle and forge authenticated requests to the backend API. The fallback tokens ('dev-token', 'fallback-token') at lines 67, 74, 102 are also problematic as they suggest the backend might accept weak authentication. HMAC secrets must remain server-side only.

**Recommendation:** CRITICAL FIX REQUIRED: (1) Remove VITE_SECRET_KEY from frontend environment variables entirely. (2) Redesign authentication to use JWT tokens issued by the backend after Supabase authentication, NOT client-side HMAC generation. (3) Backend should verify the user's Supabase JWT and issue a short-lived backend session token. (4) Remove generateBackendAuthHeader from frontend - authentication headers should come from backend-issued tokens. (5) Remove all fallback authentication modes ('dev-token', 'fallback-token') - these create security bypasses. (6) Rotate HMAC secret immediately. This vulnerability allows complete API authentication bypass.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding SEC-002 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Broken separation of concerns - hooks contain business logic and data transformation
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Broken separation of concerns - hooks contain business logic and data transformation
requirements: The established architecture (CLAUDE.md lines 234-248) defines clear layer responsibilities: Hooks should 'encapsulate data operations and state management', Services should handle 'business logic and data processing', and API layer should handle 'direct API communication'. However, useCustomer.js violates this by implementing extensive business logic within the hook: environment detection (lines 93, 142, 174), conditional data processing (lines 99-113, 147-154, 186-194), and direct service function calls for transformations. The hook imports 10+ service functions (lines 12-20) and uses them conditionally based on environment, which should be handled at the service layer. This creates tight coupling between presentation logic and business logic, making the hook difficult to test and reuse.

**Recommendation:** Refactor to proper layered architecture: (1) Move all environment detection and conditional logic to the service layer. Create a single service function like `fetchAndProcessCustomers(options)` in customerService.js that handles environment detection internally and returns consistently formatted data. (2) Hook should only call one service function and manage React state, not choose between multiple processing functions. (3) Remove all ENVIRONMENT_TYPES imports from hooks - environment concerns belong in services. (4) Pattern should be: `const result = await customerService.fetchCustomers(options); setCustomers(result.customers); setPagination(result.pagination);` with NO conditional logic in the hook.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding ARC-001 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Inconsistent architectural patterns across similar hooks
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Inconsistent architectural patterns across similar hooks
requirements: The codebase shows three different architectural patterns for the same type of operation across hooks: (1) useCustomer.js (lines 88-128) uses environment detection IN the hook with conditional service calls, (2) useProject.js (lines 63-91) hardcodes 'backend' format and calls processProjectData with source parameter, avoiding environment detection entirely, and (3) useNote.js (lines 1-100) delegates ALL business logic to the service layer with no environment handling. This inconsistency violates the architectural principle that similar operations should follow similar patterns. The established pattern per CLAUDE.md is that hooks should be thin wrappers around service calls, yet we have three radically different implementations.

**Recommendation:** Standardize on Pattern C (useNote pattern) as the correct architecture: (1) All hooks should delegate to service layer without environment detection. (2) Services handle environment concerns internally. (3) Update useCustomer.js to follow useNote.js pattern: remove getEnvironmentContext, remove conditional logic, call single service function. (4) Update useProject.js to remove hardcoded 'backend' parameter - service should detect environment internally. (5) Create architectural decision record (ADR) documenting the standard hook pattern to prevent future inconsistencies. (6) Code review checklist should verify hooks don't contain business logic.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding ARC-002 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Service layer violates single responsibility - transformBackendToFileMaker breaks abstraction
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Service layer violates single responsibility - transformBackendToFileMaker breaks abstraction
requirements: The service layer architecture (CLAUDE.md line 239-242) should provide 'business logic and data processing' with 'API abstraction', yet customerService.js implements transformBackendToFileMaker (lines 34, 50, 100) which transforms backend data TO a deprecated FileMaker format. This violates single responsibility because: (1) The migration is complete (TSK0025 done), FileMaker is removed, yet services still output FileMaker format. (2) Services should provide domain models, not legacy wire formats. (3) The transformation happens AFTER the API call, meaning every consumer receives FileMaker-formatted data even in a pure backend architecture. (4) This creates unnecessary coupling to deprecated formats in the business layer.

**Recommendation:** Eliminate FileMaker format from service layer outputs: (1) Remove all transformBackendToFileMaker calls from processBackendCustomerList and processBackendCustomerDetail. (2) Services should return data in the backend/domain model format (business_name, is_active, etc.), not legacy FileMaker format (Name, f_active). (3) If any components require FileMaker format for backward compatibility, create a presentation-layer adapter/transformer in the component directory, not the service layer. (4) Update all consuming hooks/components to use backend field names. (5) Delete transformBackendToFileMaker utility after migration is verified complete. This aligns services with their defined responsibility of business logic, not format translation.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding ARC-003 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] API layer bypasses service layer - direct imports violate layered architecture
tasks.title: [REVIEW] API layer bypasses service layer - direct imports violate layered architecture
requirements: The documented architecture (CLAUDE.md line 420) shows the flow as 'Component → Hook → Service → API Client → Backend API', establishing a strict layered architecture where each layer only communicates with adjacent layers. However, useCustomer.js (lines 3-10) imports API functions directly: `import { fetchCustomers, fetchCustomerById, ... } from '../api'`, bypassing the service layer entirely. The hook then calls API functions directly (line 94: `await fetchCustomers(options)`) and THEN calls service functions for transformation. This violates the architecture in two ways: (1) Hooks should not import from API layer - they should only import from services. (2) The pattern 'call API, then call service to process' inverts the dependency - services should encapsulate API calls.

**Recommendation:** Enforce strict layered architecture: (1) Remove ALL API imports from hooks. Hooks should only import from '../services'. (2) Create service functions that encapsulate the full operation: `customerService.fetchCustomers(options)` should call the API internally and return processed data. (3) Hook pattern should be: `import { fetchCustomers } from '../services/customerService'; const customers = await fetchCustomers(options);` - ONE function call that returns ready-to-use data. (4) Update CLAUDE.md to explicitly prohibit cross-layer imports. (5) Add ESLint rule to prevent hooks from importing from '../api' directory. This ensures proper separation of concerns and makes the codebase easier to test and modify.

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding ARC-004 from iteration 1

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Dual-signature anti-pattern in useNote violates interface segregation
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Dual-signature anti-pattern in useNote violates interface segregation
requirements: useNote.js handleNoteCreate (lines 63-78) implements a 'dual-signature' pattern supporting both new signature (entityType, entityId, content, type) and legacy signature (entityId, content, type). The function uses complex runtime detection to determine which signature was used. This violates the Interface Segregation Principle and creates maintenance burden: (1) The migration is complete (TSK0025), there should be no 'legacy' code. (2) Runtime signature detection (lines 67-78) adds cognitive load and performance overhead. (3) Comments admit this is for 'backward compatibility' but don't specify what code uses the legacy signature. (4) This pattern will persist indefinitely unless explicitly removed.

**Recommendation:** Eliminate dual-signature pattern immediately: (1) Search codebase for ALL calls to handleNoteCreate and update to new signature. (2) Remove the signature detection logic (lines 67-78) and support ONLY the new signature. (3) Update JSDoc to remove 'backward compatibility' documentation. (4) If any calling code cannot be updated, create a separate deprecated function `handleNoteCreate_LEGACY` that calls the new signature internally, with deprecation warning. (5) This is a post-migration cleanup that should have been part of TSK0025. (6) Add to coding standards: 'No dual-signature functions - use function overloading via separate exports or default parameters for optional args'.
